user_query:
We’re a volunteer group tracking narratives about wrongful convictions to plan awareness campaigns. Please build a small, reproducible trend check in this workspace that (1) uses our local config and code hints, (2) runs live web search queries, and (3) outputs structured artifacts we can review.

Inputs to use (inspect all of them first):
- scripts/analyzer.py (inspect this to apply the canonical_domains mapping when grouping results by site)
- input/config.yaml (inspect this for query templates, general keywords, disallowed domains, and output directory)
- data/watchlist.csv (names to watch)
- data/history.jsonl (previously seen links to deduplicate against)

Task requirements:
1) Construct queries by combining each template in input/config.yaml["queries"] with each name in data/watchlist.csv. Also run a set of general queries for each item in input/config.yaml["general_keywords"]. Use a general-purpose search engine for all queries (no APIs or logins required). Log every query you run with an ISO-8601 timestamp to output/queries.log (one query per line: timestamp<TAB>query).
2) For each query, collect 5–10 distinct search results if available (title and URL at minimum). Extract the hostname from each URL as the domain. Map that domain to a canonical group using scripts/analyzer.py’s canonical_domains mapping; if a domain is not present in the mapping, use the domain itself as the domain_group.
3) Filter out any result where the (canonicalized) domain_group is in input/config.yaml["disallowed_domains"], and filter out any URL that already exists in data/history.jsonl (exact URL match, case-insensitive). Keep only the remaining items.
4) Save all new, filtered results to output/new_results.jsonl as JSON Lines. Each line must be a JSON object with keys: query (string), watchlist_term (string for name-based queries; null for general keyword queries), title (string), url (string), domain (string), domain_group (string), fetched_at (ISO-8601 timestamp when you collected it).
5) From output/new_results.jsonl, generate output/trend_summary.csv with the header: term_type,term,new_links,unique_domains,top_domain_group,top_domain_new_links. For term_type, use "watchlist" for name-based queries and "keyword" for general keywords. For each name and each general keyword, compute: (a) total count of new links, (b) the number of unique domain_groups, (c) the domain_group with the most new links for that term (break ties arbitrarily), and (d) the count for that top domain_group.
6) Produce a brief high-level summary at output/brief.md that includes: (a) total candidates collected before filtering, (b) total new links after filtering and deduping, (c) the top 3 watchlist terms by new link count with their counts, and (d) the top 3 domain_groups by new link count across all terms. Keep it concise.

Constraints and checks:
- You must use the canonical_domains mapping in scripts/analyzer.py to populate domain_group consistently; this makes code inspection essential.
- You must read input/config.yaml and data/watchlist.csv to build queries and apply disallowed domain filtering; this makes configuration and file inspection essential.
- You must read data/history.jsonl to deduplicate; this makes directory inspection essential.
- Do not include any filtered-out or previously seen URLs in output/new_results.jsonl.
- All outputs must be created under the workspace at the exact paths: output/new_results.jsonl, output/trend_summary.csv, output/queries.log, and output/brief.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
